compiled_state.title: Reducer cache serves stale state after same-size in-place event rewrite
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Summary
Reducer cache key is filename+size only, so a same-size in-place rewrite of an event file serves stale state.

## Reproduction Steps
(verified on installed 0.4.0 == HEAD, temp store)
1. rebar comment <id> "AAAA".
2. Overwrite that event file's body so the comment reads "BBBB" (same byte length).
3. rebar show <id> -> still "AAAA" until any other event lands.

## Expected vs Actual
- Expected: reads reflect on-disk events.
- Actual: compute_dir_hash (ticket_reducer/_cache.py:38-57) hashes name:size (+ .archived marker) only; the docstring even scopes detection to "same filename, different size".

## Impact
git checkout/rebase of the tickets branch, fsck-recover cherry-picks, or any equal-length rewrite serves stale reads through show/list/search (library, CLI, MCP).

## Source
src/rebar/_engine/ticket_reducer/_cache.py:38-57.

## Fix direction
Include st_mtime_ns in the hash parts (stat is already performed for size; expect ~zero overhead — see validation notes comment for measurement). Content hash is the stronger alternative if mtime granularity worries arise.

## Suggested RED test
Same-size overwrite -> fresh state on next read; cache still hits when nothing changed.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Same-size in-place rewrite invalidates the cache (RED test first)
- [ ] No measurable read-path regression (cache still effective on unchanged dirs)
- [ ] Docstring updated to the new invariant

comments.body:
IMPLEMENTATION NOTES (experimentally validated in sandbox, 2026-06-09):
Validated diff (ticket_reducer/_cache.py compute_dir_hash): replace os.path.getsize with one os.stat read:
    st = os.stat(path); size, mtime_ns = st.st_size, st.st_mtime_ns
    (OSError fallback: size, mtime_ns = -1, -1)
    hash_parts.append(f"{name}:{size}:{mtime_ns}")
Validated: same-byte-length rewrite now detected (baseline stale). Perf on a 200-ticket x 3-event store: cold 0.082s->0.070s, warm-avg 0.0143s->0.0145s — overhead indistinguishable from noise (getsize already stats). Hash format is NOT pinned by tests (grep: only test_ticket_reducer.py ~2749-3112 asserting marker sensitivity/determinism, all pass: 69 passed). Determinism preserved (mtime stable between calls). One-time effect: all caches recompute once after upgrade (harmless).
COORDINATE with bug vocal-jig-apron (reducer version key in the same function) — land as one change to avoid double cache flushes.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
